speaker: PANDARUS
text: How now? Where’s thy master? At my

speaker: PANDARUS
text: cousin Cressida’s?

speaker: MAN
text: No, sir, he stays for you to conduct him thither.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: O, here he comes.—How now, how now?

speaker: TROILUS
text: Sirrah, walk off.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Have you seen my cousin?

speaker: TROILUS
text: No, Pandarus. I stalk about her door

speaker: TROILUS
text: Like a strange soul upon the Stygian banks

speaker: TROILUS
text: Staying for waftage. O, be thou my Charon,

speaker: TROILUS
text: And give me swift transportance to those fields

speaker: TROILUS
text: Where I may wallow in the lily beds

speaker: TROILUS
text: Proposed for the deserver! O, gentle Pandar,

speaker: TROILUS
text: From Cupid’s shoulder pluck his painted wings

speaker: TROILUS
text: And fly with me to Cressid!

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Walk here i’ th’ orchard. I’ll bring her

speaker: PANDARUS
text: straight.

speaker: TROILUS
text: I am giddy; expectation whirls me round.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Th’ imaginary relish is so sweet

speaker: TROILUS
text: That it enchants my sense. What will it be

speaker: TROILUS
text: When that the wat’ry palate taste indeed

speaker: TROILUS
text: Love’s thrice-repurèd nectar? Death, I fear me,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Swooning destruction, or some joy too fine,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Too subtle-potent, tuned too sharp in sweetness

speaker: TROILUS
text: For the capacity of my ruder powers.

speaker: TROILUS
text: I fear it much; and I do fear besides

speaker: TROILUS
text: That I shall lose distinction in my joys,

speaker: TROILUS
text: As doth a battle when they charge on heaps

speaker: TROILUS
text: The enemy flying.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: She’s making her ready; she’ll come straight.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: You must be witty now. She does so blush and

speaker: PANDARUS
text: fetches her wind so short as if she were frayed with

speaker: PANDARUS
text: a spirit. I’ll fetch her. It is the prettiest villain. She

speaker: PANDARUS
text: fetches her breath as short as a new-ta’en sparrow.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Even such a passion doth embrace my bosom.

speaker: TROILUS
text: My heart beats thicker than a feverous pulse,

speaker: TROILUS
text: And all my powers do their bestowing lose,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Like vassalage at unawares encount’ring

speaker: TROILUS
text: The eye of majesty.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Come, come, what need you

speaker: PANDARUS
text: blush? Shame’s a baby.—Here she is now. Swear

speaker: PANDARUS
text: the oaths now to her that you have sworn to me.

speaker: PANDARUS
text:
Cressida
                 
                offers
                 
                to
                 
                leave
                .
               What, are you gone again?

speaker: PANDARUS
text: You must be watched ere you be made tame, must

speaker: PANDARUS
text: you? Come your ways; come your ways. An you

speaker: PANDARUS
text: draw backward, we’ll put you i’ th’ thills.—Why

speaker: PANDARUS
text: do you not speak to her?—Come, draw this curtain

speaker: PANDARUS
text: and let’s see your picture.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Alas the day, how loath you are to offend daylight!

speaker: PANDARUS
text: An ’twere dark, you’d close sooner.—So, so, rub on,

speaker: PANDARUS
text:
and kiss the mistress. 
                (
                They
                 
                kiss
                .
              ) How now? A

speaker: PANDARUS
text: kiss in fee-farm? Build there, carpenter; the air is

speaker: PANDARUS
text: sweet. Nay, you shall fight your hearts out ere I

speaker: PANDARUS
text: part you. The falcon as the tercel, for all the ducks

speaker: PANDARUS
text: i’ th’ river. Go to, go to.

speaker: TROILUS
text: You have bereft me of all words, lady.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Words pay no debts; give her deeds. But

speaker: PANDARUS
text: she’ll bereave you o’ th’ deeds too, if she call your

speaker: PANDARUS
text:
activity in question.
                 
                (
                They
                 
                kiss
                .
              ) What, billing

speaker: PANDARUS
text: again? Here’s “In witness whereof the parties

speaker: PANDARUS
text: interchangeably—.” Come in, come in. I’ll go get a fire.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Will you walk in, my lord?

speaker: TROILUS
text: O Cressid, how often have I wished me thus!

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: “Wished,” my lord? The gods grant—O, my

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: lord!

speaker: TROILUS
text: What should they grant? What makes this

speaker: TROILUS
text: pretty abruption? What too-curious dreg espies

speaker: TROILUS
text: my sweet lady in the fountain of our love?

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: More dregs than water, if my fears have eyes.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Fears make devils of cherubins; they never

speaker: TROILUS
text: see truly.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Blind fear, that seeing reason leads, finds

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: safer footing than blind reason, stumbling without

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: fear. To fear the worst oft cures the worse.

speaker: TROILUS
text: O, let my lady apprehend no fear. In all

speaker: TROILUS
text: Cupid’s pageant there is presented no monster.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Nor nothing monstrous neither?

speaker: TROILUS
text: Nothing but our undertakings, when we vow

speaker: TROILUS
text: to weep seas, live in fire, eat rocks, tame tigers,

speaker: TROILUS
text: thinking it harder for our mistress to devise imposition

speaker: TROILUS
text: enough than for us to undergo any difficulty

speaker: TROILUS
text: imposed. This is the monstruosity in love, lady, that

speaker: TROILUS
text: the will is infinite and the execution confined, that

speaker: TROILUS
text: the desire is boundless and the act a slave to limit.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: They say all lovers swear more performance

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: than they are able and yet reserve an ability that

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: they never perform, vowing more than the perfection

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: of ten and discharging less than the tenth part

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: of one. They that have the voice of lions and the

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: act of hares, are they not monsters?

speaker: TROILUS
text: Are there such? Such are not we. Praise us as

speaker: TROILUS
text: we are tasted, allow us as we prove; our head shall

speaker: TROILUS
text: go bare till merit crown it. No perfection in reversion

speaker: TROILUS
text: shall have a praise in present. We will not

speaker: TROILUS
text: name desert before his birth, and, being born, his

speaker: TROILUS
text: addition shall be humble. Few words to fair faith.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Troilus shall be such to Cressid as what envy can

speaker: TROILUS
text: say worst shall be a mock for his truth, and what

speaker: TROILUS
text: truth can speak truest not truer than Troilus.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Will you walk in, my lord?

speaker: PANDARUS
text: What, blushing still? Have you not done

speaker: PANDARUS
text: talking yet?

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Well, uncle, what folly I commit I dedicate

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: to you.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: I thank you for that. If my lord get a boy of

speaker: PANDARUS
text: you, you’ll give him me. Be true to my lord. If he

speaker: PANDARUS
text: flinch, chide me for it.

speaker: TROILUS
text: You know now your hostages:

speaker: TROILUS
text: your uncle’s word and my firm faith.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Nay, I’ll give my word for her too. Our kindred,

speaker: PANDARUS
text: though they be long ere they be wooed, they

speaker: PANDARUS
text: are constant being won. They are burrs, I can tell

speaker: PANDARUS
text: you; they’ll stick where they are thrown.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Boldness comes to me now and brings me heart.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Prince Troilus, I have loved you night and day

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: For many weary months.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Why was my Cressid then so hard to win?

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Hard to seem won; but I was won, my lord,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: With the first glance that ever—pardon me;

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: If I confess much, you will play the tyrant.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: I love you now, but till now not so much

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: But I might master it. In faith, I lie;

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: My thoughts were like unbridled children grown

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Too headstrong for their mother. See, we fools!

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Why have I blabbed? Who shall be true to us

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: When we are so unsecret to ourselves?

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: But though I loved you well, I wooed you not;

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: And yet, good faith, I wished myself a man;

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Or that we women had men’s privilege

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Of speaking first. Sweet, bid me hold my tongue,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: For in this rapture I shall surely speak

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: The thing I shall repent. See, see, your silence,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Cunning in dumbness, from my weakness draws

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: My very soul of counsel! Stop my mouth.

speaker: TROILUS
text: And shall, albeit sweet music issues thence.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Pretty, i’ faith!

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: My lord, I do beseech you pardon me.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: ’Twas not my purpose thus to beg a kiss.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: I am ashamed. O heavens, what have I done!

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: For this time will I take my leave, my lord.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Your leave, sweet Cressid?

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Leave? An you take leave till tomorrow

speaker: PANDARUS
text: morning—

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Pray you, content you.

speaker: TROILUS
text: What offends you, lady?

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Sir, mine own company.

speaker: TROILUS
text: You cannot shun yourself.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Let me go and try.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: I have a kind of self resides with you,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: But an unkind self that itself will leave

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: To be another’s fool. I would be gone.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Where is my wit? I know not what I speak.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Well know they what they speak that speak so wisely.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Perchance, my lord, I show more craft than love

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: And fell so roundly to a large confession

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: To angle for your thoughts. But you are wise,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Or else you love not; for to be wise and love

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Exceeds man’s might. That dwells with gods above.

speaker: TROILUS
text: O, that I thought it could be in a woman—

speaker: TROILUS
text: As, if it can, I will presume in you—

speaker: TROILUS
text: To feed for aye her lamp and flames of love,

speaker: TROILUS
text: To keep her constancy in plight and youth,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Outliving beauty’s outward, with a mind

speaker: TROILUS
text: That doth renew swifter than blood decays!

speaker: TROILUS
text: Or that persuasion could but thus convince me

speaker: TROILUS
text: That my integrity and truth to you

speaker: TROILUS
text: Might be affronted with the match and weight

speaker: TROILUS
text: Of such a winnowed purity in love;

speaker: TROILUS
text: How were I then uplifted! But, alas,

speaker: TROILUS
text: I am as true as truth’s simplicity

speaker: TROILUS
text: And simpler than the infancy of truth.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: In that I’ll war with you.

speaker: TROILUS
text: O virtuous fight,

speaker: TROILUS
text: When right with right wars who shall be most right!

speaker: TROILUS
text: True swains in love shall in the world to come

speaker: TROILUS
text: Approve their truth by Troilus. When their rhymes,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Full of protest, of oath and big compare,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Wants similes, truth tired with iteration—

speaker: TROILUS
text: “As true as steel, as plantage to the moon,

speaker: TROILUS
text: As sun to day, as turtle to her mate,

speaker: TROILUS
text: As iron to adamant, as Earth to th’ center”—

speaker: TROILUS
text: Yet, after all comparisons of truth,

speaker: TROILUS
text: As truth’s authentic author to be cited,

speaker: TROILUS
text: “As true as Troilus” shall crown up the verse

speaker: TROILUS
text: And sanctify the numbers.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Prophet may you be!

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: If I be false or swerve a hair from truth,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: When time is old and hath forgot itself,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: When water drops have worn the stones of Troy

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: And blind oblivion swallowed cities up,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: And mighty states characterless are grated

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: To dusty nothing, yet let memory,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: From false to false, among false maids in love,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Upbraid my falsehood! When they’ve said “as false

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: As air, as water, wind or sandy earth,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: As fox to lamb, or wolf to heifer’s calf,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Pard to the hind, or stepdame to her son,”

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Yea, let them say, to stick the heart of falsehood,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: “As false as Cressid.”

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Go to, a bargain made. Seal it, seal it. I’ll be

speaker: PANDARUS
text: the witness. Here I hold your hand, here my

speaker: PANDARUS
text: cousin’s. If ever you prove false one to another, since

speaker: PANDARUS
text: I have taken such pains to bring you together, let

speaker: PANDARUS
text: all pitiful goers-between be called to the world’s

speaker: PANDARUS
text: end after my name: call them all panders. Let all

speaker: PANDARUS
text: constant men be Troiluses, all false women Cressids,

speaker: PANDARUS
text: and all brokers-between panders. Say “Amen.”

speaker: TROILUS
text: Amen.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Amen.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Amen. Whereupon I will show you a chamber

speaker: PANDARUS
text: with a bed, which bed, because it shall not

speaker: PANDARUS
text: speak of your pretty encounters, press it to death.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Away.

speaker: PANDARUS
text: And Cupid grant all tongue-tied maidens here

speaker: PANDARUS
text: Bed, chamber, pander to provide this gear.
